006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.oidc.rp;
018
019import java.time.*;
020import java.util.*;
021
022import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.oauth.*;
024
025/**
026 * Immutable record describing a logged-in OpenID Connect Relying Party session.
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Created by {@link OidcRelyingParty#completeLogin} after a successful login and persisted via a
030 * {@link SessionStore}.  The {@link #principal()} carries the verified ID-token claims and is what the
031 * {@link OidcSessionAuthFilter} surfaces to {@code RoleBasedRestGuard} / {@code @Auth Principal}.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * Two fields support server-side (back-channel) revocation: {@link #subject()} (the {@code sub} claim)
035 * and {@link #sid()} (the optional {@code sid} session-id claim).  A server-side-indexed
036 * {@link SessionStore} keys on these so an IdP-pushed logout token can invalidate the matching
037 * session(s).
038 *
039 * @param id The opaque server-side session id.  For {@link InMemorySessionStore} this is the cache key;
040 *    for {@link SignedCookieSessionStore} it is a stable identifier embedded in the signed cookie.  Never
041 *    {@code null}.
042 * @param subject The ID-token {@code sub} claim.  Never {@code null} or blank.
043 * @param sid The ID-token {@code sid} (session id) claim, if the IdP issued one.  Used for back-channel
044 *    logout targeting a single session rather than all of a subject's sessions.
045 * @param principal The verified ID-token claims as a {@link ClaimsPrincipal}.  Never {@code null}.
046 * @param roles The roles extracted from the configured roles claim.  Never {@code null}; may be empty.
047 * @param token The tokens returned from the token endpoint (access / refresh / id), if the store retains
048 *    them.  {@link SignedCookieSessionStore} does NOT retain tokens (stateless cookies don't carry them),
049 *    so this is {@link Optional#empty()} for cookie-backed sessions.
050 * @param createdAt The instant the session was created.  Never {@code null}.
051 * @param expiresAt The instant the session expires (bounded independently of token lifetime).  Never
052 *    {@code null}.
053 * @since 10.0.0
054 */
055public record OidcSession(
056      String id,
057      String subject,
058      Optional<String> sid,
059      ClaimsPrincipal principal,
060      Set<String> roles,
061      Optional<OAuthToken> token,
062      Instant createdAt,
063      Instant expiresAt) {
064
065   /**
066    * Compact constructor enforcing non-null fields and defensively copying the role set.
067    */
068   public OidcSession {
069      Objects.requireNonNull(id, "id");
070      Objects.requireNonNull(subject, "subject");
071      Objects.requireNonNull(sid, "sid");
072      Objects.requireNonNull(principal, "principal");
073      Objects.requireNonNull(token, "token");
074      Objects.requireNonNull(createdAt, "createdAt");
075      Objects.requireNonNull(expiresAt, "expiresAt");
076      roles = roles == null
077         ? Collections.emptySet()
078         : Collections.unmodifiableSet(new LinkedHashSet<>(roles));
079   }
080
081   /**
082    * Returns whether this session is expired relative to the supplied instant.
083    *
084    * @param now The reference instant.
085    * @return <jk>true</jk> if {@code now} is at or after {@link #expiresAt()}.
086    */
087   public boolean isExpired(Instant now) {
088      Objects.requireNonNull(now, "now");
089      return !now.isBefore(expiresAt);
090   }
091}
